title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Execution Trace: Tls Listener Identity
summary: A public server uses package defaults, has zero timeouts, trusts unlimited headers, enables cleartext HTTP/2 accidentally, serves weak TLS, and terminates active requests on deploy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo tls listener identity increment in explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure TLS identity, versions, certificates, ALPN, rotation, proxy termination, redirects, and plaintext policy with end-to-end scheme evidence.
Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Tls Listener Identity · Theory Tls Listener Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1460 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure TLS identity, versions, certificates, ALPN, rotation, proxy termination, redirects, and plaintext policy with end-to-end scheme evidence.
